subject: Stop-Work Order Posted on My Garage Project – 714 Morris Ave
body_clean: Good morning, Mayor Okonkwo. I came home Tuesday to find a stop-work order stapled to my garage door at 714 Morris Ave. My contractor pulled what I was told was a proper building permit back in August, and the work is over halfway finished. Nobody called us, nobody sent a letter first. Is there a process to appeal or at least find out what specifically triggered the order? I'm paying my contractor to sit idle and winter is coming fast. Any help getting this sorted quickly would mean a lot.

subject: Stop-Work Order Posted on My Garage Project – 714 Morris Ave
body_clean: Ms. Holmberg, thank you for reaching out to the Mayor's office. I've pulled the permit record for 714 Morris Ave and looped in our Code Enforcement division. It appears the stop-work notice was issued because the permit on file was for a roof repair, not a full structural garage rebuild — the scope of work visible on site exceeded what was approved. Sandra Pulaski from Code Enforcement will contact you by end of day Thursday with the specific citations and the steps to either amend the existing permit or obtain a supplemental one. We want to get you moving again before the cold sets in.

subject: Stop-Work Order Posted on My Garage Project – 714 Morris Ave
body_clean: Thank you so much, Tina. Sandra reached out this morning and was very clear about what needs to be corrected on the permit application. My contractor is submitting the amended drawings today and Sandra said inspections can resume once the revised permit is approved, which she estimated at about five business days. I really appreciate how fast the Mayor's office moved on this.

subject: Certificate of Occupancy for 2208 Bellwood Ave – Still Waiting
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I completed the conversion of my two-flat at 2208 Bellwood Ave back in late August and passed all my rough inspections. My final inspection was scheduled for September 12th but the inspector never showed and I haven't been able to reach anyone to reschedule. My tenants are ready to move in and I'm losing rent money every week this drags on. Can someone please look into why my CO is stalled? Permit number is BP-2025-0441.

subject: Certificate of Occupancy for 2208 Bellwood Ave – Still Waiting
body_clean: Mr. Oguike, thank you for bringing this to my attention and I apologize for the frustrating delay. A missed inspection appointment with no follow-up is not acceptable and I've personally flagged BP-2025-0441 with our building department supervisor this afternoon. You should receive a call to schedule a new final inspection within 24 hours. I understand there's real money on the table here and we will move this forward promptly.

subject: Neighbor Built a Deck Without a Permit – 418 Frederick Ave
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I live next door to 418 Frederick Ave and this summer my neighbor put up a large deck — must be 400 square feet — with no permit posted anywhere and no inspectors coming around. It's right on the property line and I'm worried it encroaches on my yard. Now they're building what looks like a pergola on top of it. I've tried talking to them but they brush me off. Can Code Enforcement come look at this before the snow covers everything up?

subject: Neighbor Built a Deck Without a Permit – 418 Frederick Ave
body_clean: Ms. Zawadzki, this is Sandra Pulaski from Code Enforcement. We conducted a site visit at 418 Frederick Ave this afternoon. No permit of record exists for the deck or the pergola structure currently under construction. I have issued a Notice of Violation under Village Ordinance 10-4-12 and served it on the property owner, who has 10 days to either obtain a retroactive permit with an as-built survey or begin removal. A setback review is included. I will keep the complaint file open and follow up at the 10-day deadline.

subject: Question About Shed Permit Threshold – 2917 Harvard Ave
body_clean: Hi Mayor, quick question before I start a project. I want to put up a storage shed in my backyard at 2917 Harvard Ave — about 10 by 12 feet, prefab, no electricity. My neighbor says anything under 120 square feet does not need a permit in Bellwood but I don't want to take any chances. Can someone confirm whether I need one or not? Don't want to end up with a violation notice come spring.

subject: Question About Shed Permit Threshold – 2917 Harvard Ave
body_clean: Mr. Thibodeau, great question and smart to check first. Under Bellwood's current zoning code, an accessory structure under 100 square feet on a residential lot does not require a building permit, but it must still meet setback requirements — minimum 3 feet from the rear and side property lines. Your 10 x 12 shed is 120 square feet and falls above the threshold, so a permit is required. You can apply at the Village Hall building counter at 3200 Washington Blvd or online through the permit portal. The fee for a structure this size is nominal and review turnaround is typically quick this time of year.

subject: Bought a House – Unpermitted Basement Finish – What Now?
body_clean: Hi Mayor Okonkwo, I just closed on a house at 738 St. Paul Ave in September and my home inspector flagged that the basement was finished without a permit — probably 10 or 15 years ago. The previous owner is long gone. My real estate attorney says I need to deal with it as the new owner. I'm not trying to hide anything. What is the Village's process for legalizing a previously unpermitted finish? I want to do this the right way but I also don't want to have to gut the whole thing.

subject: Bought a House – Unpermitted Basement Finish – What Now?
body_clean: Ms. Radziwill, congratulations on your new home and thank you for coming forward proactively. For a pre-existing unpermitted finish, Bellwood's process is to apply for a retroactive permit. An inspector will visit to assess the work visible without demolition; for concealed elements like electrical wiring, a licensed electrician's inspection report may be required. If the work meets current code, we issue the permit and close it out. If there are deficiencies, you'll get a punch list — you will not automatically have to gut everything. I'd suggest calling the building department to schedule a pre-application consultation first; they're very helpful in scoping these situations.

subject: Permit Application Submitted 6 Weeks Ago – No Response
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I submitted a permit application on August 4th for a bathroom addition at 1103 Granville Ave and it has now been six weeks with no approval, no rejection, and no status update when I call the permit desk. The person I speak to says it is in review every single time. I had a contractor lined up who has since moved on to other jobs. At what point does a permit application actually get reviewed? Is there a standard timeline? I am beyond frustrated.

subject: Permit Fee Waiver for Veteran Homeowner – 509 Hirsch Ave
body_clean: Hello Mayor Okonkwo, I'm a disabled veteran and I'm planning a ramp addition at the front of my home at 509 Hirsch Ave to improve accessibility. I've heard some municipalities offer permit fee waivers for accessibility modifications for veterans or people with disabilities. Does Bellwood have any such program? I'm on a fixed income and the contractor quote is already stretching my budget. I'd really appreciate any information you can share.

subject: Code Violation Notice on My Corner Lot Fence – 3301 Washington Blvd
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I received a code violation notice last week saying my fence at 3301 Washington Blvd exceeds the permitted height on a corner lot. I installed this fence three years ago and nobody said a word until now. The notice says I have 30 days to bring it into compliance. The fence is 6 feet tall and from what I read online, 6 feet is allowed on residential property. What am I missing here? Do I have any recourse to appeal before I start tearing out posts?

subject: Possible Unpermitted Room Addition at 1622 Englewood Ave
body_clean: Mayor, something has been bugging me for weeks. The property directly behind mine at 1622 Englewood Ave has been under construction — looks like they're framing out a full room addition on the back of the house. I have never seen a permit posted, and activity has been going on off and on since early October. I don't want to be a bad neighbor but if something is not permitted and there's a fire or a structural problem, I don't want anyone getting hurt. Can Code Enforcement take a look?

subject: Re-Inspection Scheduled Weeks Out – 851 Bohland Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm at a loss. My inspector failed the electrical rough-in at 851 Bohland Ave on September 22nd and cited three items. My licensed electrician corrected all three and I requested a re-inspection September 26th. It's now October 3rd and no one has come. When I call, they say the re-inspection queue is backed up two to three weeks. My framing is open and I cannot insulate or drywall until this passes. Is there anything your office can do to move this along?

subject: Permit for Driveway Approach Widening – 2034 Rice Ave
body_clean: Hello, I live at 2034 Rice Ave and would like to widen my driveway approach. The concrete company I hired says I need a permit but could not tell me whether it's a Village permit, a county permit, or something else. I'm also not sure if there's any IDOT involvement since Rice Ave might be a different type of road. Can someone point me in the right direction before I spend money on plans?

subject: Stop-Work on Roofing Project – Contractor Did Not Pull Permit – 2756 Monroe St
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo. I hired a roofing company to replace my roof at 2756 Monroe St and they got hit with a stop-work order on day two. Turns out they never pulled a permit before starting — that is on them, not me. My whole roof is stripped and tarped and rain is in the forecast. The contractor says they cannot restart until the permit clears, which could be a week. Is there any way the Village can expedite the permit review given the emergency of an open roof in late October?

subject: Is My Kitchen Remodel Permit Still Valid? – 1209 Madison St
body_clean: Hi, I got a permit for a kitchen remodel at 1209 Madison St in March 2025 but then I had health issues over the summer and couldn't start the project. It's now November. I know permits expire but I can't remember if mine said 6 months or 12 months. I would hate to lose the permit and the fee I already paid. Can someone look up my permit and tell me if it's still active, and if not, what renewal costs?

subject: Received Final Notice for Permit I Did Not Open – 419 25th Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I received a final notice letter this week threatening fines for an open permit at my property at 419 25th Ave. Problem is, I never applied for any permit. After some digging I believe it was opened by the previous owner around 2019 for a water heater replacement and never closed out. I bought the house in 2021 and this is the first I'm hearing about it. Can your office help me understand whether I'm actually liable for something I didn't create?

subject: Dispute With Inspector Over Egress Window Size – 3128 Bellwood Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm finishing my basement at 3128 Bellwood Ave and my inspector is requiring a larger egress window in the bedroom I'm creating. My contractor says the 5.8 square foot opening meets IRC minimum. The inspector says Bellwood adopted an amendment requiring 6.0 square feet. I cannot find this amendment anywhere in writing. Is there a way to review the Village's adopted local building code amendments, or is there a formal appeals process for inspection disputes?

subject: Certificate of Occupancy Needed Before My Closing – 917 Marshall Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I am trying to wrap up a single-family home conversion at 917 Marshall Ave. Everything passed — framing, electrical, mechanical, plumbing. My final building inspection was approved October 8th. But I still do not have a certificate of occupancy and my title company says I cannot close the sale without it. The permit desk says it is a processing backlog. My closing is scheduled November 14th. I am begging someone to help. I can provide the inspection report if that helps move things along.
